signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Jobs Data
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.8708 — NZD leads G8 performance while CAD collapses following softer-than-expected Canadian employment data. The 1.601% G8 spread indicates a high-conviction bilateral theme. RBNZ 2.25% vs BoC 2.25% (0bp) carry is neutral, but RBNZ hike bias (↑) vs BoC hold (→) drives the divergence. Downside CAD momentum accelerates as job market weakness validates BoC's hold stance.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Geopolitical + ECB Inflation Warning
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1777 — ECB's Lagarde warns climbing energy costs from Strait of Hormuz clashes will push up input prices, keeping rate hikes in focus despite war-driven uncertainty. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (175bp USD carry advantage) structurally limits upside, but EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation (corr=+0.720 vs norm=-0.125, z=+1.78σ) indicates EUR is trading as a proxy for energy-driven inflation risk rather than equity sentiment. Sustained break above 1.1800 requires further hawkish ECB repricing.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7245 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates institutional conviction, while retail sentiment is 88% short. This extreme divergence suggests retail is fading the trend, creating capitulation risk if price extends higher. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% (60bp AUD carry premium) supports the long bias. Watch for retail short-covering to accelerate the move toward 0.7300.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.67 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite US 10Y yields softening to 4.364%. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 remains crowded, leaving the pair vulnerable to sharp unwinds if geopolitical tensions in the Middle East escalate further. 160.00 remains the key level for BoJ verbal warnings; carry dominates the regime while VIX remains at 17.2 (normal volatility).

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Sentiment Extreme
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7771 — Retail sentiment is 85% long, diverging from COT LF net short -6,117. Retail is attempting to catch the bottom of a 1M -3.00% decline. Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0% (375bp USD carry premium) favors the long side, but persistent CHF safe-haven demand amid Strait of Hormuz clashes keeps price pressured. Retail capitulation risk rises if 0.7750 fails to hold.
